feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
entry.content: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
entry.content: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

entry.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
entry.content: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

entry.title: Judge orders release of more than $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse, defamation case
entry.content: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

entry.title: Guardians, former Padres reliever leaves MLB to play in South Korea
entry.content: The Guardians released the right-hander to let him pursue the opportunity.

entry.title: Sneak peek: My Mother's Murder Trials
entry.content: ENCORE: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports in a two-part "48 Hours" airing Saturday, July 11 from 9-11 p.m. ET on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

entry.title: Trump says he's not sure he wants to make a deal with Iran
entry.content: President Trump on Wednesday said he wasn't sure he wants to make a deal with Iran and threatened more strikes against the country. CBS News' Nancy Cordes reports and Robert B. Murrett, professor of practice at Syracuse University, joins with analysis.

entry.title: Boomer Parents: It's Time to Cough it Up | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
entry.content:
Americans have been anxiously awaiting the so-called "great wealth transfer," where trillions of dollars are expected to pass down from Baby Boomers to their kids. But people are living longer and holding onto their assets. Jill says it's time to start opening up the purse strings now. Plus, caller questions: Toby asks whether sterling silver jewelry is actually a worthwhile investment, and Dave wants to know if taking a pay cut to improve his quality of life is financially realistic.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

entry.title: Trump hails "unity" at NATO summit, despite recent tensions
entry.content: President Trump concluded the NATO summit with a press conference touting the "tremendous love" exhibited by the allies.

entry.title: Phillies' Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub gets worse amid Justin Verlander news
entry.content: This All-Star snub for Zack Wheeler looks even worse now for MLB.

entry.title: Mammoth make Barrett Hayton contract decision after Devils offer sheet
entry.content: Utah and New Jersey were waiting for this moment.

entry.title: Tigers' Justin Verlander makes retirement announcement amid All-Star news
entry.content: The future Hall of Famer has made a big decision.

entry.title: Is Folarin Balogun playing against Belgium? Latest updates on USMNT star’s red card suspension and appeal
entry.content: The latest updates on USA star Folarin Balogun's status for the Round of 16 game against Belgium.

entry.title: Carli Lloyd annihilates Christian Pulisic with 9-word critique after USA's World Cup-ending loss
entry.content: Pulisic is getting a lot of criticism right now.

entry.title: Why U.S. men split equally $16 million World Cup prize with USWNT
entry.content: It's a headline that is catching attention after the United States bowed out of the World Cup.

entry.title: Luka Doncic has one feeling for Lakers future after LeBron James exit
entry.content: With LeBron James moving on, the Lakers instantly built a brand-new roster around Luka Doncic and the superstar guard is completely bought in.

entry.title: Trump faces questions on conflict with Iran during NATO summit press conference
entry.content: President Trump held a news conference capping off the latest NATO summit in Turkey and faced questions from reporters about the ongoing conflict with Iran. NBC News' Garrett Haake and Courtney Kube report on the latest developments in negotiations and strikes in the Middle East.

entry.title: This Glaucoma Therapy Company Is Keeping An Eye On Profits
entry.content: This Glaucoma Therapy Company Is Keeping An Eye On Profits

entry.title: Stocks, bonds retreat after Trump says Iran MOU 'is over'
entry.content: Stocks, bonds retreat after Trump says Iran MOU 'is over'

entry.title: CGI Inc. (GIB) and NetApp Join Forces to Modernize IT Infrastructure and Improve Data Management
entry.content: CGI Inc. (GIB) and NetApp Join Forces to Modernize IT Infrastructure and Improve Data Management

entry.title: Sony (SONY) Moves Further Into Digital Gaming With PS3, PS Vita Store Closures
entry.content: Sony (SONY) Moves Further Into Digital Gaming With PS3, PS Vita Store Closures

entry.title: SpaceX stock hovers at IPO opening price as rival Blue Origin seeks $10 billion in fresh funding
entry.content: SpaceX stock hovers at IPO opening price as rival Blue Origin seeks $10 billion in fresh funding

entry.title: Kentucky governor urges McConnell to be ‘transparent’ and give an update on his health
entry.content: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear is asking Mitch McConnell for an update on his health and whether he believes he can continue to serve in the role.

entry.title: From The Sports Desk: Argentina’s great escape
entry.content: Lionel Messi scored another goal — his eighth of the tournament — to help his team to one of the great World Cup comebacks, a 3-2 win over Egypt despite trailing by two with only 11 minutes of regulation time to play.

entry.title: Tigers' Kevin McGonigle breaks impressive rookie record set by Aaron Judge
entry.content: This is a very impressive record for McGonigle to set in his rookie season.

entry.title: How Warriors letting Quinten Post leave gives Grizzlies incredible frontcourt depth
entry.content: Golden State and Memphis end up getting involved on this deal.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 printable bracket: Download a free quarterfinal knockout chart here
entry.content: Download The Sporting News’ printable 2026 World Cup bracket, including expert tips and predictions to help pick every game in the knockout rounds.

entry.title: Phillies' Zack Wheeler blasts MLB for All-Star snub after 14-K start: 'It pisses me off'
entry.content: Zack Wheeler has every right to be frustrated with MLB right now.

entry.title: Jayson Tatum finally breaks silence on 'tough' Jaylen Brown trade that broke up Celtics star duo
entry.content: Celtics star Jayson Tatum is speaking out about the franchise trading his longtime co-star.

entry.title: LeBron James rumors tracker: Latest news on Warriors, Cavaliers and other potential 2026-27 landing spots
entry.content: For likely the last time in his career, all eyes across the NBA are on James' decision. Here's the latest on where the basketball legend will spend his age-42 season.

entry.title: Milan plotting next steps after spending €100m on two transfers
entry.content: Milan have already committed over €100m in funds on the signings of two players at the start of the summer transfer window, but Wednesday’s reports claim that the Rossoneri will not stop there and...

entry.title: VVS Laxman to head coach for Zimbabwe tour and Asian Games; Gautam Gambhir expected to rest after England Series
entry.content: In a strategic move, VVS Laxman will lead India's white-ball team during the Zimbabwe tour and Asian Games, while Gautam Gambhir steps back after the England series. Anticipated to assist Laxman are Hrishikesh Kanitkar and Sunil Joshi. This coaching arrangement reflects India's effort to navigate a tightly-packed tournament schedule in international cricket.

entry.title: Tom Holland Names the England He’s ‘Fangirling’ Over After World Cup Heroics
entry.content: England’s impressive run at the 2026 FIFA World Cup has captured the attention of football fans across the globe, and actor Tom Holland is no exception. The Spider-Man star was recently asked which England player he has been “fangirling” over during the tournament.

entry.title: Georgia linebacker Jalon Walker named Falcons' most promising player
entry.content: Former Georgia Bulldog Jalon Walker has been named the Falcons' most promising building block by Kristopher Knox of Bleacher Report.

entry.title: Looking back at Fayetteville's MLB Draft history, school by school
entry.content: Fayetteville has seen a slew of local athletes chosen in the MLB Draft over the years. Here's a look at the complete list of local picks.

entry.title: 2026 four-star running back flips from Penn State to Big Ten rival
entry.content: 2026 four-star Aiden Gibson flipped from the Penn State Nittany Lions to the Rutgers Scarlet Knights, according to Hayes Fawcett.

entry.title: OKC Thunder drops Summer League game to Jazz as Aday Mara, Bennett Stirtz sit
entry.content: The OKC Thunder dropped its Summer League game to the Utah Jazz, as Aday Mara and Bennett Stirtz sat. Here are takeaways.

entry.title: What are the Titans playoff chances in 2026?
entry.content: The Tennessee Titans could be a surprise playoff contender in 2026 if quarterback Cam Ward makes a second year leap.

entry.title: A Country Club Built a Wall to Protect Its Golf Course—and All Hell Broke Loose
entry.content: As the 14th hole sunk into the sea, contractors erected a stone structure to protect it. Easy fix, right? Wrong.

entry.title: Chinese men in Germany used Telegram groups to share rape videos and drugging tips, prosecutors say
entry.content: A German investigation into an online predator network that thrived on the messaging app Telegram for years has led to the convictions of three alleged members of the group's inner circle and the ongoing trial of a fourth man in Berlin

entry.title: U.S. says China's nuclear weapons buildup "of great concern" after missile test
entry.content: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

entry.title: U.S. hits dozens of Iranian targets in retaliatory strikes after ship attacks
entry.content: With peace talks on hold, the U.S. said it launched a "series of powerful strikes" on Iran in response to Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: California still hasn't released Baby2Baby diaper contract as lawmakers weigh longer public records delays
entry.content: The Newsom administration waited 24 days to decide if it would even let Californians see the multi-million-dollar contract with Baby2Baby, a nonprofit linked to First Partner Jennifer Siebel Newsom.

entry.title: YouTube defends video that falsely claims Sydney massacre survivor is 'crisis actor'
entry.content: A Google executive has told an inquiry that a YouTube video that falsely claims a wounded survivor of an antisemitic massacre in Sydney is a crisis actor blooded with makeup met the platform’s standards and would remain online

entry.title: Unstable building in Midtown forces evacuations, street closures
entry.content: The unstable building is the former Pfizer headquarters in Midtown Manhattan that is being converted into apartments.

entry.title: Democrats Stevens and El-Sayed spar in tense Michigan Senate debate
entry.content: The debate comes days after a third candidate, Mallory McMorrow, suspended her campaign.

entry.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
entry.content: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

entry.title: License of Utah school where Paris Hilton alleged she was abused is revoked
entry.content: Utah has revoked the license of a boarding school where Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager, saying the school has "failed to provide applicable health and safety services for clients."

entry.title: U.S. launches strikes against Iran over attacks on 3 ships in Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has over the past two days attacked three commercial vessels that were traveling through the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Southern Poverty Law Center pleads not guilty in federal fraud case
entry.content: The Southern Poverty Law Center, best known for monitoring hate groups, pleaded not guilty Tuesday to federal charges that it defrauded donors by not disclosing that some of the money would be used to pay informants in extremist organizations.

entry.title: Argentina’s World Cup comeback
entry.content: Lionel Messi and Argentina defeated Egypt today after trailing behind them for much of the game. The team scored three goals in under 15 minutes. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch has the story.

entry.title: Phillies' Kyle Schwarber closing in on Babe Ruth's MLB record
entry.content: Schwarber leads MLB in home runs so far this season.

entry.title: Who is still in the World Cup? Updated list of teams left in FIFA Round of 16 bracket
entry.content: As the World Cup bracket narrows, here are the teams still competing for a championship.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Fear is interest paid on a debt you may not owe.
Lucky Numbers: 50, 60, 75, 41, 17, 43

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use a pants hanger to hold cookbooks in the kitchen.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Babies are born without knee caps. They don't appear until the child reaches 2-6 years of age.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Steve Maraboli
entry.content: “Letting go means to come to the realization that some people are a part of your history, but not a part of your destiny.” — Steve Maraboli

entry.title: Egypt's controversial overturned goal sparks Argentina rigged World Cup firestorm
entry.content: It's certainly a tough one to watch back and make sense of.

entry.title: How Mets scored 3 runs on crazy 'Little League home run' as Royals implode
entry.content: This is one of the craziest plays you'll see the entire MLB season.

entry.title: LeBron James taking Philadelphia 76ers 'very seriously' as potential landing spot
entry.content: Philadelphia, Cleveland and Miami appear to be James' top options.

entry.title: 76ers, Heat, Cavaliers reportedly in bidding war for $101M four-time NBA champion, former MVP
entry.content: The Philadelphia 76ers, Miami Heat, and Cleveland Cavaliers are all in a great spot for the biggest free agent remaining. Any team is going to be lucky to have this veteran.

entry.title: Celtics officially outbid Knicks, Lakers for historic 7-foot rebounding stud, NBA champion
entry.content: The Boston Celtics are bolstering their frontcourt to begin the offseason.

entry.title: FIFA World Cup schedule 2026: Complete match dates, times, team fixtures for soccer tournament
entry.content: The World Cup is going big for the 2026 edition of soccer's most illustrious tournament, expanding to 48 teams for the first time ever in the joint-hosted tournament held in USA, Canada, and Mexico.

entry.title: Meta built an AI detection tool to ID images and video created with its new models
entry.content: Meta's new AI detector has rate limits for some reason.

entry.title: Thousands of women could be spared painful cancer exam by new NHS AI blood test
entry.content: The test, which is being trialled by two NHS trusts, could replace the transvaginal ultrasound scan in some casesThousands of women could be spared having a painful intrusive exam for suspected cancer thanks to a new AI-powered blood test being trialled by the NHS.Around 90,000 postmenopausal women a year in England are referred by their GP to be investigated for possible womb cancer because they are bleeding a lot. Continue reading...

entry.title: The next Samsung Galaxy Unpacked launch is happening on July 22
entry.content: The next Samsung Galaxy Unpacked launch is happening on July 22.

entry.title: Immigration agent fatally shot a man in Houston during an enforcement operation, authorities say
entry.content: A U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer shot and killed a man in Houston after he attempted to evade arrest in his vehicle during an operation Tuesday, the agency said.

entry.title: World Cup quarterfinal match schedule, television times
entry.content: The World Cup is going see its quarterfinals start on Thursday, July 9, and continue through July 11 when there will be two matches.

entry.title: 5 big things we learned about UCF football at Big 12 media days
entry.content: Scott Frost, Alonza Barnett III, Preston Cushman, Lewis Carter and Jayden Bellamy represented the UCF Knights at Big 12 media days in Frisco, Texas.

entry.title: Eight remain! All you need to know about the 2026 World Cup quarter-finals
entry.content: Tuesday (7) brought the round of 16 of the 2026 FIFA World Cup to a close.Only eight teams remain in the tournament, battling for football’s most coveted trophy.With that in mind, OneFootball brings...

entry.title: World Cup 2026: Switzerland heads to the quarterfinals after beating Colombia on penalty kicks
entry.content: Switzerland is in the quarterfinals for the first time since 1954.

entry.title: As defending champs, why aren't Seahawks commanding our attention?
entry.content: The lack of flashy moves is a key ingredient in this under-discussed narrative. No headline-grabbing trades or departures, just steady internal growth and subtle front-office moves.

entry.title: ‘Complacency isn’t in their vocabulary’: BYU football insists it can handle lofty expectations in 2026
entry.content: With expectations soaring for the 2026 college football season, BYU coaches and players were featured heavily at the Big 12's football media day

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: 2026 World Cup quarterfinals: Confirmed schedule, results, predictions
entry.content: Here's all of the confirmed matches for the World Cup quarterfinals, with predictions on every game.

entry.title: U.S. airlines' monthly fuel spending topped $6 billion again in May, up 84% from year ago
entry.content: U.S. airlines spent $6.66 billion on jet fuel in May, the second straight month that fuel costs topped $6 billion, according to government data released Tuesday.

entry.title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
entry.content: If you're building something ambitious, this is a fast track to the people who can move your startup forward.

entry.title: Meta rolls out Muse, a new AI image generator
entry.content: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising, decorating and creator-based opportunities.

entry.title: U.S. Launches Strikes on Iran in Response to Ship Attacks in Hormuz
entry.content: The action comes after Iran attacked commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a peace deal agreed to end the U.S.-Iran war.

entry.title: NASA transfers ownership of Maryland woodland to the US Fish and Wildlife Service
entry.content: A rare 2026 story of good news for the planet.

entry.title: Traders on Kalshi think the Nasdaq-100 will end 2026 above 30,000, predicting a cooler second half of the year
entry.content: Speculators think the massive rally in the Nasdaq-100 since the lows following the U.S.-Iran war will be more subdued in the second half of 2026.

entry.title: Meta's new Muse Image model accepts Instagram accounts as a prompt
entry.content: The new AI model is also powering effects in Stories and image generation in WhatsApp.

entry.title: McConnell speaks to GOP leaders as speculation swirls about health, hospitalization
entry.content: Senate Republican leaders have spoken to Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell as he remains in the hospital

entry.title: Mavericks miss out on proven floor-spacer after Clippers swoop in during free agency
entry.content: The Mavericks missed out on shooter Rui Hachimura after he signed a $28M deal with the Clippers, leaving Dallas to look for trades to fix its frontcourt.

entry.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
entry.content: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

entry.title: Twins get 'absolute haul' Joe Ryan trade update from MLB insider
entry.content: This is the kind of update that might have the Twins more open to a trade.

entry.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
entry.content: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

entry.title: Switzerland vs. Colombia live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup knockout game
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for Switzerland vs. Colombia in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Round of 16 match in Vancouver.

entry.title: Astros make telling decision on 5.99 ERA starter after offseason Pirates trade
entry.content: Houston is battling for a postseason spot, and this pitcher was doing more harm than good.

entry.title: Lionel Messi's best World Cup goals: Where Argentina soccer legend's heroics vs. Egypt ranks all-time
entry.content: The Sporting News ranks Messi's greatest-ever World Cup goals, from his first goal against Serbia and Montenegro to his latest strike against Egypt.

entry.title: Broncos named as landing spot for ex-Steelers, Dolphins 3,529-yard Pro Bowl TE
entry.content: This potential "Joker" free-agent TE option would make a lot of sense for the Broncos.

entry.title: Insider offers trade theory on why Dalton Knecht is unplayable with Lakers
entry.content: A Los Angeles Lakers pundit didn't mince his words on the franchise's declining 25-year-old guard.

entry.title: What Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are actually doing with wedding footage
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tied the knot on July 3.

entry.title: A Messi Miracle Takes Over the World Cup
entry.content: Argentina makes stunning comeback in round-of-16 game against Egypt

entry.title: Texas Tech coach Joey McGuire on why Brendan Sorsby saga won't change transfer approach
entry.content: Joey McGuire and Texas Tech have found too much success in the transfer portal to overhaul everything in the wake of the Brendan Sorsby scandal,

entry.title: FIFA condemns racist abuse toward IShowSpeed at World Cup match
entry.content: IShowSpeed was livestreaming the game and could be heard asking a fan in an Argentina jersey what was being said toward him

entry.title: FIFA condemns racist abuse toward IShowSpeed at World Cup match
entry.content: IShowSpeed was livestreaming the game and could be heard asking a fan in an Argentina jersey what was being said toward him

entry.title: Atlanta Braves released MLB veteran pitcher in favor of Owen Murphy before Pirates series
entry.content: The Atlanta Braves have made another wave of roster moves.

entry.title: 'Choice is quite obvious': Ab de Villiers fears Vaibhav Sooryavanshi may choose 'easier' T20 format over Test cricket
entry.content: Speaking on TOI Sports' Bombay Sports Exchange Podcast, de Villiers said the growing appeal of T20 cricket could influence young players like Sooryavanshi when deciding the direction of their careers.

entry.title: Johan Manzambi out with injury for Switzerland in round of 16 against Colombia at the World Cup
entry.content: Switzerland standout Johan Manzambi will miss the team’s round-of-16 match at the World Cup against Colombia on Tuesday with an injury. Manzambi was one of three Swiss players who left training early Monday in the team’s final preparations before Tuesday’s match. The other two players, Ruben Vargas and Djibril Sow, were available off the bench.
